text:
is a Japanese anime production company, best known for its work on the Rebuild of Evangelion film series in conjunction with GAINAX. Commonly known as , it is the primary animation production studio for the films, and the sole copyright holder for the Neon Genesis Evangelion franchise after GAINAX suffered major debt. https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2024-06-07/anime-studio-gainax-files-for-bankruptcy/.211643 https://web.archive.org/web/20161119051644/http://diamond.jp/articles/-/108195?page=2 It was founded by Hideaki Anno on 17 May 2006, and was first mentioned publicly on 01 August 2006 when recruitment notices were posted on Anno's website; Anno remains its president. http://web.archive.org/20100718101128/khara.weblogs.jp/hideakianno/personal-biography.html It has offices in West Shinjuku.

The name khara comes from the Greek word χαρα , meaning joy.

While Anno was one of the founding members of GAINAX, http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2006-08-07/new-hideaki-anno-project-in-the-works he seems to be leaving it and heading khara; as part of his public statements on the Rebuild films, he wrote:
 "For this purpose, we are not returning to our roots at Gainax. I have set up a production company and studio, and it is in this new setting that we will start again. Without looking back, without admiration for the circumstances, we aim to walk towards the future. Thankfully, we have gathered staff from the old series, new staff, and many other fantastic staff to work on this series. We realize that we are creating something that will be better than the last series." Hideaki Anno Releases Statement About New Evangelion Movies - Anime News Network
